channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israeli forces seize documents that reveal Hamas plan for more elaborate attacks: report
item.description: Hundreds of documents seized by Israeli forces reveal a broader ambition by Hamas to plot attacks against Israel in the years before Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez criticizes Biden administration over 'unrestrained' Israel government
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) criticized the Biden administration on Monday over what she called an “unrestrained” Israeli government.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Why US is sending troops, prized air defense system to Israel
item.description: The U.S. announced this week a major new air defense system for Israel, along with the deployment of 100 American troops to operate it in the country, in a development that will bolster Israeli security but potentially put American forces at risk.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: A bus carrying university students crashes, killing 12 and injuring 33 in Egypt's northeast
item.description: The Egyptian health ministry says a bus crashed and overturned on a highway in northeastern Egypt, killing 12 people and injuring 33 others.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Italy sends the first ship with migrants to Albania as rights groups warn of a dangerous precedent
item.description: Italy is transferring the first group of migrants to Albania as part of a contentious plan to process thousands of asylum-seekers outside its borders.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russia says it captured a southern Ukraine village in a push before winter comes
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia said Monday it captured the village of Levadne in southern Ukraine as it probes for weaknesses along the war ’s roughly 1,000-kilometer (600-mile) front line, including in eastern areas that are the main focus of Moscow’s military effort before winter arrives.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hezbollah's drones are a fierce and evasive threat to Israel
item.description: A single drone, which evaded Israel’s vaunted aerial defense system, slammed into a mess hall at a military training camp deep inside Israel, killing four soldiers and wounding dozens of others.
item.dc:creator: Tia Goldenberg, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: India-Canada diplomatic war intensifies over killing probe
item.description: India has strongly rejected Canada's allegations that its diplomats are "persons of interest" in a murder investigation, stating that the claims are part of Trudeau's "political agenda" and that Canada had failed to provide evidence to support its claims.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, The Hill

item.title: North Koreans fighting in Ukraine alongside Russians, Zelensky says
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said Sunday that North Korean troops are now fighting alongside Russian forces in Ukraine, appearing to confirm reports that have been circulating in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Poland's leader defends his decision to suspend the right to asylum
item.description: Human and civil rights groups are expressing concern after Poland's leader said he would suspend the right to asylum because of its alleged abuse by eastern neighbors Belarus and Russia.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Gera, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: French citizen convicted in Russia of collecting military information and gets 3 years in prison
item.description: A Russian court has convicted a French citizen of collecting information on military issues and sentenced him to three years in prison.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Netanyahu mulls plan to empty northern Gaza of civilians and cut off aid to those left inside
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is examining a plan to seal off humanitarian aid to northern Gaza in an attempt to starve out Hamas militants, a plan that, if implemented, could trap without food or water hundreds of thousands of Palestinians unwilling or unable to leave t
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: EU condemns attacks on UN peacekeepers in Lebanon
item.description: The European Union has condemned attacks on U.N. peacekeepers in Lebanon and rejected Israeli allegations that the U.N. was keeping them there to obstruct military operations against Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israeli strike in northern Lebanon kills at least 21 people
item.description: The Lebanese Red Cross says an Israeli airstrike hit an apartment building in northern Lebanon, killing at least 21 people.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa And Samy Magdy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ailing and silenced in prison, Belarus activist symbolizes the nation's repression
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — The last time any of Maria Kolesnikova's family had contact with the imprisoned Belarusian opposition activist was more than 18 months ago. Fellow inmates at the penal colony reported hearing her plead for medical help from inside her tiny and smelly cell.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: US says it will send THAAD defense battery to Israel, along with US troops
item.description: The U.S. is sending a Terminal High Altitude Area Defense battery to Israel, along with US troops to operate it. The Pentagon made the announcement just hours after Iran warned the U.S. to keep its military forces out of Israel.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine’s human rights envoy urges response to alleged killings of Ukrainian POWs in Kursk
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine’s human rights ombudsman urged international organizations Sunday to respond to a claim that several Ukrainian prisoners of war were executed in Russia’s Kursk region, where Kyiv had launched an incursion in August.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea says front-line units ready to strike South Korea if more drones appear
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea said Sunday its front-line army units are ready to launch strikes on South Korea, ramping up pressure on its rival that it said flew drones and dropped leaflets over its capital Pyongyang.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-NATO chief warns Iran-Israel conflict could 'shut down oil coming out of Middle East'
item.description: James Stavridis, former NATO Chief Admiral, joined the Cats Roundtable radio show Sunday morning to weigh in on the impact of extreme conflict in Israel. The former leader said the rising tensions in the region “could […] shut down oil coming out of the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Indian politician known for his close ties with Bollywood is killed in Mumbai
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — A senior politician in India’s financial capital, Mumbai, who was also known for his close ties with Bollywood has been shot dead weeks before a key state election.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lithuanians vote in parliamentary election with many looking for change despite a strong economy
item.description: VILNIUS, Lithuania (AP) — Lithuanians voted Sunday in the first round of parliamentary elections that could lead to the center-right governing coalition being replaced by the opposition Social Democrats and smaller center-left parties.
item.dc:creator: LIUDAS DAPKUS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden: Israel should 'absolutely' stop strikes on UN peacekeepers in Lebanon
item.description: President Biden said Friday that Israel should “absolutely” stop strikes on the U.N. peacekeepers in Lebanon, following two incidents the group was involved in this week.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Navy SEALs' drownings in Iranian op were preventable, investigation finds
item.description: An investigation into the death of two Navy SEALS who drowned during a mission to seize Iranian missile parts bound for the Houthi rebels in Yemen found that the deaths could have been prevented.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Israeli forces kill 2 Lebanese soldiers and injure 2 UN peacekeepers in separate strikes
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — An Israeli airstrike killed two Lebanese soldiers and wounded three on Friday, Lebanon's military said, just hours after the Israeli military fired on the headquarters of U.N. peacekeepers in southern Lebanon, injuring two of them for the second day in a row.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia launches 4th aerial attack in a week against Ukraine's grain-exporting Odesa region
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A nighttime Russian missile strike on Odesa killed at least four people including a 16-year-old girl, regional authorities said Friday, in the latest in a series of attacks this week on the southern Ukrainian region that are likely intended to disrupt the country’s grain exports
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Iran's national airliner accused of flying weapons to Beirut airport for terror proxy Hezbollah: Report
item.description: Iran’s domestic airline, already sanctioned for support to Russia, is being accused of aiding Hezbollah by an Iranian dissident group who has called for additional sanctions to be put on the carrier.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea vows to block border with South Korea and build front-line defense structures
item.description: To cope with 'confrontational hysteria' by U.S. and South Korean forces, North Korea said it will block its border with South Korea permanently and will cut off railways and roads to the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea's Kim again threatens to use nuclear weapons against South Korea and US
item.description: The latest warning came as experts say North Korea could ramp up hostilities ahead of the U.S. presidential election.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Former hostage wants ‘peace in the world’ one year later
item.description: One year later, the Chicago-area woman is steadfast in her faith and hopeful others captured that day will be released.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation
